story.iWant: Capture One .costyle format fully integrated into Recipe's CLI, TUI, and web interfaces
story.soThat: I can convert Capture One presets using my preferred interface (command line, terminal UI, or browser) with the same seamless experience as other supported formats (XMP, lrtemplate, NP3)
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Update Converter Package (AC-5) - Add "costyle" and "costylepack" format constants - Update Convert() to handle Capture One formats (parse/generate routing) - Handle .costylepack batch conversions (unpack → convert → pack) - Import costyle package
### Task 2: Update Format Detection (AC-4) - Add .costyle/.costylepack extension detection - Add XML magic byte detection for .costyle - Add ZIP magic byte detection for .costylepack - Validate Capture One XML structure
### Task 3: CLI Integration (AC-1) - Update help text with Capture One examples - Test CLI conversion commands - Verify verbose output logs Capture One steps - Verify JSON output includes Capture One metadata
### Task 4: TUI Integration (AC-2) - Add Capture One format to menu (purple badge) - Update file browser for .costyle files - Update preview screen for Capture One parameters - Test batch mode with .costylepack bundles
### Task 5: Web Integration (AC-3) - Add .costyle detection in format-detection.js - Accept .costyle/.costylepack uploads in file-upload.js - Display Capture One parameters in parameter-preview.js - Handle .costyle conversions in conversion.js - Add Capture One to target format selector
### Task 6: Documentation Updates (AC-6) - Update README.md with Capture One format - Update web landing page with Capture One badge - Add Capture One FAQ entry - Update format compatibility matrix - Update CHANGELOG.md
### Task 7: Error Handling (AC-7) - Implement Capture One-specific error messages - Add error display in CLI/TUI/Web - Test error paths (corrupt files, unsupported parameters)
### Task 8: Integration Testing (AC-8) - CLI integration test (convert .costyle to all formats) - TUI integration test (navigate, convert) - Web integration test (upload, convert, download) - Batch test (.costylepack bundles) - Round-trip test (CLI → Web → CLI consistency)
### Task 9: Final Validation (AC-1 to AC-8) - Manual testing across all interfaces - Verify consistent behavior (CLI vs TUI vs Web) - Performance check (conversion times)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**AC-1: CLI Integration** - CLI accepts .costyle files: ecipe convert input.costyle --to xmp - CLI accepts .costylepack bundles: ecipe convert bundle.costylepack --to xmp-bundle - Format auto-detection works for .costyle and .costylepack - Help text includes Capture One examples - Verbose output logs Capture One-specific steps - JSON output includes Capture One format metadata
**AC-2: TUI Integration** - TUI format menu includes "Capture One (.costyle)" option - Format badge uses purple color (Capture One brand) - File browser detects and displays .costyle files - Batch mode supports .costylepack bundles - Preview screen displays Capture One parameters - Progress indicators work for .costylepack batch conversions
**AC-3: Web Integration** - Web UI accepts .costyle uploads via drag-and-drop - Web UI accepts .costylepack uploads (batch conversion) - Format detection identifies .costyle files automatically - Parameter preview displays Capture One adjustments - Target format selector includes Capture One option - Download triggers for generated .costyle files
**AC-4: Format Detection** - Detect .costyle by extension - Detect .costylepack by extension - Validate XML structure (xmpmeta, RDF, Description) - Return format type "costyle" or "costylepack" - Format detection works in CLI, TUI, Web contexts - Handle ambiguous files gracefully
**AC-5: Converter Integration** - Converter.Convert() handles "costyle" format type - Route to costyle.Parse() for input files - Route to costyle.Generate() for output files - Handle .costylepack bundles (unpack → convert → pack) - Support all conversion paths (costyle ↔ xmp/np3/lrtemplate/dcp) - Preserve bundle structure for pack conversions
**AC-6: Documentation Updates** - README.md includes Capture One in supported formats - CLI help text includes .costyle examples - Web FAQ includes Capture One format questions - Parameter mapping docs include .costyle mappings - Format compatibility matrix includes Capture One - Deployment docs note Capture One support
**AC-7: Error Handling** - Invalid .costyle XML → clear error message - Corrupt .costylepack ZIP → clear error message - Unsupported parameters → warning logged, conversion continues - Empty .costyle → convert to neutral preset - CLI/TUI/Web display Capture One errors consistently
**AC-8: Integration Testing** - CLI integration test: Convert .costyle to all formats - TUI integration test: Navigate, convert .costyle file - Web integration test: Upload .costyle, convert, download - Batch test: Convert .costylepack bundle - Round-trip test: Verify consistency across interfaces

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Capture One Format Support
doc.snippet: Components: New Package internal/formats/costyle/ with parse.go, generate.go, types.go, pack.go. Integration Points: Extends internal/converter/converter.go to recognize .costyle format, adds Capture One format badge to web UI, updates parameter-mapping.md

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Capture One Format Support
doc.snippet: Converter.Convert() extended to handle costyle format in switch statements. Parse: costyle.Parse(data). Generate: costyle.Generate(recipe). Format detection checks XML header + xmpmeta namespace + crs:Exposure tag

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Capture One Format Support
doc.snippet: CLI: recipe convert input.costyle --to xmp. TUI: Format menu includes Capture One with purple badge. Web: Upload .costyle via drag-drop, convert to other formats. Format detection automatically identifies .costyle files. Help text and documentation updated

doc.title: Parameter Mapping Rules
doc.snippet: Capture One → UniversalRecipe mapping: Exposure (direct), Contrast (-100..100 → -1.0..1.0), Saturation, Temperature, Tint, Clarity. Color balance for shadows/midtones/highlights with hue/saturation

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: CLI Interface
doc.snippet: cmd/cli/format.go: Format detection utilities. cmd/cli/convert.go: Convert command implementation. internal/converter: Shared conversion engine (single source of truth). Thin CLI layer, all business logic in internal/converter

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: TUI Interface
doc.snippet: TUI format menu with color-coded badges. File browser filters for preset formats (.np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate). detectFileFormat() returns format string based on extension. Preview screen displays parameters

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.section: Technical Decisions - Decision 3: File Format Auto-Detection
doc.snippet: Auto-detect via content inspection (WASM). User uploads file → detectFormat(bytes). Display detected format badge. WASM overhead negligible (<1ms). Epic 1's DetectFormat() proven 100% accurate

doc.title: recipe - Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: File upload via drag-and-drop. Format auto-detection. Parameter preview display. Target format selection. Conversion execution via WASM. File download. Privacy: Zero network requests during conversion

doc.title: Story 8.1: Capture One .costyle Parser
doc.snippet: Hub-and-spoke architecture: Capture One .costyle → Parse() → UniversalRecipe. Package location: internal/formats/costyle/. Zero external dependencies: encoding/xml, fmt. Data flow: Upload → costyle.Parse() → XML unmarshal → map to UniversalRecipe

doc.title: Format Compatibility Matrix
doc.snippet: Matrix showing parameter support across formats (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate). Visual indicators for full support, partial support, not supported. Parameter categories: Basic, Tone Curve, Color, Advanced

artifact.reason: Core conversion function that needs extension to handle costyle format. Switch statements for parsing (line 90-98) and generation (line 113-121) need costyle cases added
Format detection function that needs extension for .costyle XML detection (check for xmpmeta + crs: tags) and .costylepack ZIP detection (magic bytes 0x50 0x4B 0x03 0x04)
Need to add FormatCostyle and FormatCostylepack constants following existing pattern
Already implemented in Story 8-1. Will be imported and used in converter.Convert() switch statement for parsing .costyle files
Will be implemented in Story 8-2. Needs to be imported and used in converter.Convert() switch statement for generating .costyle files
Will be implemented in Story 8-3. Handles .costylepack bundle ZIP operations. Converter needs to detect and route bundle files appropriately
CLI format detection wrapper. Should delegate to converter.DetectFormat(). Verify it works correctly with new .costyle format after converter extension
CLI convert command definition. Help text (Long description) needs update to include Capture One .costyle examples
TUI format menu definitions. Need to add Capture One format entry with purple badge color following existing pattern
Web format detection JavaScript. Needs extension to detect .costyle extension and call WASM detectFormat() for content-based detection
Web file upload handler. Should already support .costyle files generically, verify no explicit file type restrictions that would block .costyle uploads
Web parameter preview display. May need extension to format Capture One-specific parameters (exposure, contrast, saturation, temperature, tint, clarity) with appropriate labels
Web target format selector dropdown. Needs to include "Capture One (.costyle)" option in format list following existing pattern

story-context.constraints: - Must maintain hub-and-spoke architecture: all conversions flow through UniversalRecipe, no direct format-to-format conversion - Must use only Go stdlib for XML/ZIP handling (no external dependencies for costyle package) - Must maintain conversion performance: single .costyle <100ms, .costylepack (50 files) <5 seconds - Must maintain ≥85% test coverage for modified code - Must preserve existing CLI/TUI/Web UX patterns and conventions - Must provide consistent error messages across all interfaces (CLI/TUI/Web) - Format detection priority: extension check first, then content inspection (magic bytes + structure validation) - Purple badge color (#9C27B0) for Capture One format in TUI and Web UI (brand consistency) - Help text must include Capture One examples in CLI (recipe convert sample.costyle --to xmp) - Documentation must be updated in README, format matrix, FAQ, CHANGELOG

interface.name: converter.Convert
interface.signature: func Convert(input []byte, from, to string) ([]byte, error)

interface.name: convert (WASM)
interface.signature: convert(inputBytes: Uint8Array, from: string, to: string) -> Promise<Uint8Array>

tests.standards: Recipe follows Go standard testing patterns with table-driven tests. Unit tests use go test with coverage target ≥85%. Integration tests verify end-to-end flows across CLI, TUI, and Web interfaces. Test files colocated with implementation (e.g., converter_test.go alongside converter.go). Benchmark tests measure performance (BenchmarkConvert). Manual validation tests generated files in actual software (e.g., load .costyle in Capture One Pro). Error handling tests cover invalid inputs, corrupt files, missing parameters. Round-trip tests verify conversion accuracy (costyle → UR → costyle maintains 95%+ parameters).

ideas.test: CLI integration test: TestCLI_ConvertCostyle - run CLI convert command with .costyle file, verify XMP output generated, check success message format, validate conversion result
CLI batch test: TestCLI_ConvertCostylepack - convert .costylepack bundle to multiple XMP files, verify all files converted, check file count matches bundle
TUI integration test: TestTUI_CostyleNavigation - verify .costyle files appear in file browser, format badge displays purple color, selection works
TUI preview test: TestTUI_CostylePreview - load .costyle in preview, verify parameters displayed (exposure, contrast, saturation, etc.)
Web integration test: TestWeb_CostyleUpload (Playwright) - drag-drop .costyle file, verify format detected, check parameter preview, convert to XMP, verify download
Web batch test: TestWeb_CostylepackUpload - upload .costylepack bundle, verify file count shown, batch convert to XMP, download all
Format detection test: TestDetectFormat_Costyle - unit test with .costyle XML content, verify "costyle" returned. Test extension-based and content-based detection
Format detection test: TestDetectFormat_Costylepack - unit test with ZIP magic bytes + .costyle entries, verify "costylepack" returned
Converter integration test: TestConvert_Costyle_AllFormats - test costyle → np3, costyle → xmp, costyle → lrtemplate, verify all paths work
Converter integration test: TestConvert_AllFormats_Costyle - test np3 → costyle, xmp → costyle, lrtemplate → costyle, verify reverse paths work
Documentation test: Manual verification - check README includes Capture One, CLI help shows examples, FAQ has entry, format matrix updated
Error handling test: TestErrorHandling_InvalidCostyle - malformed XML, verify clear error message "malformed .costyle file"
Error handling test: TestErrorHandling_CorruptCostylepack - truncated ZIP, verify clear error message "corrupt .costylepack bundle"
Round-trip test: TestRoundTrip_CrossInterface - CLI convert .costyle → .xmp, Web upload .xmp convert → .costyle, CLI convert .costyle → verify matches original (consistency check)
